title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Hercule Poirot re-examines room 238 at the Brouks Hotel, where the corpse of Richard Nigges was discovered. Although the room appears tidy, Poirot finds a loose tile in the fireplace. Removing the tile reveals a hidden key labeled "238." Based on this discovery, Poirot concludes that the killer locked the door from the inside and escaped through the window, hiding the key behind the tile. He observes that while the killer took the keys to rooms 317 and 121, they left Nigges's key behind, suggesting Nigges was treated differently than the other victims.

Captain Calver provides additional details regarding the three victims: Harriet Sipper, Ada Gransberry, and Richard Nigges. All three traveled to London by train. Nigges had pre-paid for their hotel rooms and arranged separate car rentals from the station to the hotel. While Sipper and Gransberry both resided in Gleeholm in Pigeon Valley, Nigges lived in Bideford, Devon. Calver notes the arrival sequence: Nigges arrived one hour before Sipper, and Sipper arrived two hours before Gransberry.

Calver and Poirot theorize that Nigges may have been an unwitting accomplice who helped the killer bring the women to the hotel without them knowing they would be staying at the same location. Poirot suggests investigating the victims' lives and secrets in Gleeholm as the next step. Calver also mentions that Henry Nigges, Richard's brother, is traveling from Devonshire to the hotel, and notes a lingering mystery regarding a dinner order delivered to Nigges's room, as the identity of the person who ordered or delivered it remains unknown.
